title: VSaveImage Statement
description: Saves the current frame grabber image on a disk file.
notes:
VSaveImage can be used to save an image to a disk file at run time. This is particularly useful for analyzing images where parts were not found.

The image that is saved is the currently displayed image. The RuntimeFreeze property should be set to True for the sequence that you are saving the image from.

When you are using the Compact Vision, USB memory can be specified in filename. When selecting the USB memory, specify it as follows: CVUSB:\path\filename.

When the saveGraphics parameter is set to "True", the image detection result will be saved with the object result (Default: false).

However, there is a possibility that the sequence processing cannot be completed immediately after VRun is executed against the sequence in which AsyncMode Property is set to False.  The image and the result graphics are saved surely if wating for the completion of the sequence processing by calling VGet and VSaveImage is called.
examples:
VRun seq1
VGet seq1.AllFound, found
If found = FALSE Then VSaveImage seq1, "c:\badimage\seq1.bmp"
EndIf
